title: [FIRRTL] Initialization of Async RegReset is different from SFC
body:
We are currently missing post-randomization for async registers done by SFC. This is causing weird divergence in simulations between SFC and MFC. 
https://github.com/chipsalliance/firrtl/blob/2eddce1a40709381b4ae9cc952708e7d6aa7b969/src/main/scala/firrtl/backends/verilog/VerilogEmitter.scala#L633-L644

For example, we have to insert extra initialization for `sync_0` and `sync_1` registers in the following program.
```scala
circuit AsyncShift:  
  module AsyncShift:
    input clock : Clock
    input reset : AsyncReset
    output io : { flip d : UInt<1>, q : UInt<1>}

    reg sync_0 : UInt<1>, clock with :
      reset => (reset, UInt<1>("h0")) 
    reg sync_1 : UInt<1>, clock with :
      reset => (reset, UInt<1>("h0")) 
    node _sync_2_T = bits(io.d, 0, 0) 
    sync_1 <= _sync_2_T 
    sync_0 <= sync_1 
    io.q <= sync_0
```

MFC:
```
`INIT_RANDOM_PROLOG_      // foo.fir:7:5
      `ifdef RANDOMIZE_REG_INIT // foo.fir:7:5
        _RANDOM = {`RANDOM};    // foo.fir:7:5
        sync_0 = _RANDOM[0];    // foo.fir:7:5
        sync_1 = _RANDOM[1];    // foo.fir:9:5
`endif
```

SFC:
```
`ifdef RANDOMIZE_REG_INIT
  _RAND_0 = {1{`RANDOM}};
  sync_0 = _RAND_0[0:0];
  _RAND_1 = {1{`RANDOM}};
  sync_1 = _RAND_1[0:0];
`endif // RANDOMIZE_REG_INIT
  if (reset) begin
    sync_0 = 1'h0;
  end
  if (reset) begin
    sync_1 = 1'h0;
  end
```
